content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion taught in high school language curricula frames reflection as both a passive reflection of existing input and an active intentional practice of re-examining one’s own thinking to improve communication, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be re-designed to explicitly leverage this dual core: separating passive mirroring of a model’s initial problem-solving output from active guided re-examination that mirrors how human students refine their writing through iterative self-reflection exercises.
